title: Leucanthemum serotinum
intro: Leucanthemum serotinum, also known as late daisy or autumn daisy, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant is used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Leucanthemum serotinum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Late daisy is typically used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
taxonomy: Leucanthemum serotinum is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It is native to Europe and Western Asia. It is further classified as a member of the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants.
